name: Philippines (presidential standard)

colors.symbolism: the sun, stars and the seal's rays — courage and patriotism

description: A blue field bearing in the centre the Seal of the President of the Philippines — a sun of eight rays above three small five-pointed stars, encircled by a ring inscribed 'SEAL OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES' between two laurel sprays, all on a roundel.
history: The presidential standard was authorised under Executive Order No. 310 (1950) and has been refined over subsequent administrations; the current emblem and proportions are set by Executive Order No. 310 and its amendments. The flag is reserved exclusively for the head of state.

features.role: Seal of the President of the Philippines
features.symbolism: the sun of liberty above three stars representing Luzon, Visayas and Mindanao, encircled by laurel and the seal's legend
